data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S4 30b3: Attested signing); re-confirmation]

Audited this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56, S2 70c7, S3 fa49, S5 b7fa, S6 1ba5). This child is the signing/attestation slice; the union of children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. EMPTY G3 findings list.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this child's role):
- AC1 (no attested gate reads working tree; all read snapshot at pinned SHA) — FULLY: S1 (materialize) + S3 (re-rooting; its AC1/AC4/AC5). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child (consumes the SHA; "Out: gate parameter threading (S3)"). Not an absence.
- AC2 (signed verdict records verified_at_sha + reproducible over identical content) — FULLY by THIS child as PRIMARY OWNER (its AC1: attested PASS records verified_at_sha + signs over it; re-run reproduces over identical content), on S1's immutable-SHA basis. Heart of this child. Clean.
- AC3 (local reads in-place checkout AND cannot sign) — SPLIT: THIS child owns "cannot sign" (its AC3: local/dirty never emits a signature); S3 owns "reads in-place checkout" (its AC4). Clean enforcer/reader split; no uncovered half.
- AC4 (concurrent gates, no git-lock contention / cross-op corruption; atomic population; delete-on-last-close) — FULLY by S1 (atomic, no-worktree) + S2 (single-flight, delete-on-last-close). This child's own AC2 (atomic+idempotent verified_at_sha pin, overwrite-on-reverify) REINFORCES, not substitutes. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- AC5 (cache + janitor reclaim without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY by S2 (sole owner). STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC6 (private-repo fetch failure -> descriptive credential error) — FULLY by S1 (raises) + S6 (documents). STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC7 (portable: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY by S1 (impl) + S6 (docs). STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC8 (all six closed; make lint + suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion; covered by existence of all six (this is #4/6) + each child's green-bar. Not an absence.

Success-criteria narrative decomposes onto AC1-AC3. "signed verdicts reproducible and bound to verified_at_sha" is precisely this child's core deliverable (its AC1; in-toto Statement envelope AC4); "unsigned local preserves verify-before-push" is reinforced by this child's signing bar (its AC3). No uncovered obligation introduced.

Named-consumer hand-off (covered-by-named-consumer, not an absence): this child's HMAC-trust-model + in-toto docs delegated to S6 ("DOCUMENTED in S6"); S6 AC4 claims it. Claim-gate ref-coherence (AC5) is in this child's own "In" scope.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing G4 dependency-gap (routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's hashing path across the S3<->S4 seam) is a CONSISTENCY concern about WHO threads the handle, not a union-coverage gap — the parent obligations (AC1 snapshot-only, AC2 reproducibility, ref-coherence) remain CLAIMED by a child (this one's AC5), so the union covers them.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the signing/attestation slice; it is the primary owner of parent AC2 and the "cannot-sign" half of AC3; no parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.
